title: Bridge Essay: Neural substrates of habit.
concept: Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.
**Phase context:** Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

**Neural substrates of habit.** — J Neurosci Res (2020). DOI: 10.1002/jnr.24552. PMID: 31693205. Active reward pursuit is supported by the balance between the cognitive and habitual control of behavior. The cognitive, goal-directed strategy relies on the prospective evaluation of anticipated consequences, which allows behavior to readily adapt when circumstances change. Repetition of successful actions promotes less cognitively taxing habits, in which behavior is automatically executed without prospective consideration. Disruption in either of these behavioral regulatory systems contributes
